enrichment.short_description: Sanity is a headless content platform (structured content CMS) that pairs a customizable editing environment called Sanity Studio with a real-time content API, used by teams to model, edit, and deliver structured content to any frontend.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium, per-seat subscription with usage-based overages
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; Growth plan starts at $15 per seat per month
enrichment.headquarters: San Francisco, California, USA (with a significant engineering presence in Oslo, Norway)
enrichment.seo_title: Sanity Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what Sanity costs in 2026, who it is for, and how its headless content platform compares. Verified pricing, features, pros, cons, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Sanity is a headless content platform founded in 2018 by Even Westvang, Magnus Kongsli Hillestad, Oyvind Rostad, and Simen Svale Skogsrud, headquartered in San Francisco, California, with a major engineering hub in Oslo, Norway, and roughly 250 to 300 employees as of 2026. The company grew out of the Oslo design and technology studio Bengler and raised a $9.3 million Series A in 2020 led by Threshold Ventures, followed by later growth rounds, positioning it as one of the best-funded independent headless CMS vendors. Sanity's core product is Sanity Studio, an open-source, React-based content editing environment that developers can fully customize, paired with a hosted, real-time content backend (the Content Lake) and a query language called GROQ for retrieving structured content. Content modeled in Sanity is delivered as structured JSON to any frontend framework, which is why it is commonly described as an API-first or composable CMS rather than a traditional page-based CMS. Sanity's pricing is freemium and seat-based: the Free plan supports up to 20 seats, two datasets, 10,000 documents, and 100GB of combined assets and bandwidth, and includes the AI-powered Content Agent and real-time collaborative editing at no cost, making it usable in production for small projects indefinitely rather than as a time-limited trial. The Growth plan costs 15 dollars per occupied seat per month (viewer-only seats are free), supports up to 50 seats and 25,000 documents across two datasets, and unlocks commenting, task management, scheduled publishing, and AI Assist, with pay-as-you-go overages for API and bandwidth usage beyond plan limits. The Enterprise plan is custom-priced and adds single sign-on, audit logs, data residency options, dedicated support, and uptime SLAs, features that are typically required only by larger, compliance-driven organizations. Sanity is widely used by editorial, e-commerce, and marketing teams that need a highly customizable content backend decoupled from presentation, and it integrates with popular frontend frameworks like Next.js, Astro, and Remix. Add-ons available on the Growth plan include dedicated support for 799 dollars per month, increased usage quotas for 299 dollars per month, and additional datasets at 999 dollars per dataset per month, while SAML SSO is bundled free with Enterprise but sold as a 1,399 dollar per month add-on on Growth. Because Sanity bills monthly with no long-term contract requirement on its self-serve tiers, it is often chosen by development teams that want CMS flexibility without vendor lock-in, though the per-seat and per-dataset cost structure means expenses can rise quickly for larger content teams or multi-brand deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Sanity is a headless, API-first content platform built around Sanity Studio, an open-source and fully customizable content editing workspace, and a hosted real-time backend called the Content Lake. Instead of coupling content to a specific website template, Sanity stores content as structured JSON that can be queried with its own query language, GROQ, and delivered to any website, app, or device.

Founded in 2018 and headquartered in San Francisco with a core engineering team in Oslo, Norway, Sanity has become a popular choice for development teams that want deep customization of their editorial workflow, real-time collaboration, and freedom from the rigid templates of traditional CMS platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Sanity Studio can be customized with React components, custom input fields, and structured content schemas defined in code, giving developers fine-grained control over the editing experience for content teams. Real-time collaborative editing lets multiple users work in the same document simultaneously, similar to a shared document editor.

The platform also includes visual editing for connecting the Studio to live frontend previews, an AI-powered Content Agent for drafting and transforming content, GROQ and GraphQL APIs for flexible querying, and asset pipelines for image and file handling with on-the-fly transformations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Sanity offers three plans billed monthly. The Free plan supports up to 20 seats, two public datasets, 10,000 documents, and 100GB of combined bandwidth and asset storage, including the Content Agent and real-time editing at no cost. The Growth plan costs 15 dollars per occupied seat per month, supports up to 50 seats and private datasets, and adds comments, tasks, scheduled drafts, and AI Assist.

The Enterprise plan is custom-priced and adds SSO, audit trails, custom usage limits, and uptime SLAs. Overages beyond plan limits are billed pay-as-you-go, for example roughly 1 dollar per 250,000 additional API CDN requests, so teams with larger content operations should budget for usage-based costs on top of seat fees.

features.title: Customizable Sanity Studio
features.description: An open-source, React-based editing environment that developers can extend with custom input components, validation, and workflows tailored to their content model.

features.title: Real-time collaborative editing
features.description: Multiple editors can work in the same document at the same time with live cursors and presence, similar to collaborative document editors.

features.title: Structured content and GROQ
features.description: Content is stored as structured JSON and queried with GROQ or GraphQL, making it reusable across websites, apps, and other channels.

features.title: AI Content Agent and AI Assist
features.description: Built-in AI tooling helps draft, translate, and transform content directly inside the Studio, available even on the Free plan for the Content Agent.

features.title: Visual editing and live preview
features.description: Editors can preview content changes against the live frontend, reducing the guesswork of editing structured content blindly.

features.title: Asset pipeline and image handling
features.description: Built-in image and file storage with on-the-fly transformations, cropping, and hotspot control for responsive delivery.

features.title: Scheduled publishing and workflow tools
features.description: Growth and Enterprise plans add scheduled drafts, tasks, and comments to support editorial review workflows.

features.title: Enterprise security and compliance
features.description: SSO, audit trails, and data residency options are available on the Enterprise plan for regulated or larger organizations.

pros: Free plan is usable in production indefinitely, not just a trial
Highly customizable editing environment for developers
Real-time collaborative editing is a differentiator versus many headless CMS competitors
Structured content model makes omnichannel delivery easier
Backed by well-known venture investors, suggesting product longevity
cons: Per-seat pricing can become expensive for larger content teams
Requires developer involvement to set up and customize the Studio, less turnkey than page-builder CMS tools
SSO is expensive as a Growth add-on, pushing compliance-focused teams toward custom Enterprise pricing
No annual billing discount on self-serve plans, only monthly billing is offered
Usage-based overages for API requests and bandwidth can add unpredictable costs

faqs.question: Is Sanity free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sanity has a genuinely free plan that supports up to 20 seats, two datasets, and 10,000 documents, and it can be used in production indefinitely rather than as a time-limited trial.

faqs.question: How does Sanity pricing work?
faqs.answer: Sanity charges per occupied seat on its Growth plan, starting at 15 dollars per seat per month, with viewer-only seats free. Enterprise pricing is custom and quoted directly by Sanity's sales team.

faqs.question: Who founded Sanity and when?
faqs.answer: Sanity was founded in 2018 by Even Westvang, Magnus Kongsli Hillestad, Oyvind Rostad, and Simen Svale Skogsrud, growing out of the Oslo studio Bengler.

faqs.question: Where is Sanity headquartered?
faqs.answer: Sanity is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with a major engineering team based in Oslo, Norway.

faqs.question: What is GROQ?
faqs.answer: GROQ is Sanity's own query language for retrieving and filtering structured content from the Content Lake, offering an alternative to GraphQL for querying Sanity data.

faqs.question: Does Sanity support real-time collaboration?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sanity Studio supports real-time collaborative editing, allowing multiple team members to edit the same document simultaneously.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Sanity Studio?
faqs.answer: Sanity Studio itself is open source and can be deployed anywhere, but the Content Lake backend, datasets, and hosted APIs are provided as a managed cloud service by Sanity.

faqs.question: What frameworks does Sanity integrate with?
faqs.answer: Sanity integrates with common frontend frameworks such as Next.js, Astro, Remix, and others via its APIs and official client libraries.

enrichment.short_description: Sastrify is a SaaS spend and vendor management platform that gives IT, procurement, and finance teams visibility into software subscriptions, automates renewal tracking, and helps negotiate lower software costs.
enrichment.pricing_model: Custom subscription pricing based on total software spend under management
enrichment.starting_price: Custom pricing; no public flat starting price (typical average annual contract value cited around $25,000)
enrichment.company_size: Approximately 55-60 employees (prior to its 2026 acquisition by Deel)
enrichment.seo_title: Sastrify Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and What It Does
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn what Sastrify costs, who it is for, and how its SaaS spend management platform works, including verified founding details and its 2026 acquisition by Deel.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Sastrify is a SaaS and cloud spend management platform founded in 2020 by Maximilian Messing and Sven Lackinger and headquartered in Cologne, Germany, with roughly 55 to 60 employees distributed across multiple continents as of 2026. The company helps IT, procurement, and finance teams track, benchmark, and optimize their software subscription portfolios, giving organizations a centralized view of every SaaS tool in use, when contracts renew, and how spend compares to market benchmarks. Sastrify's platform automates renewal reminders so companies do not get locked into unwanted auto-renewals, and it offers procurement support where Sastrify's own team assists in negotiating better pricing directly with software vendors on the customer's behalf, with the company citing average software and cloud cost reductions of 30 percent or more after adoption. The product targets mid-market and larger technology companies, particularly those with more than 100 employees and meaningful annual SaaS spend, since its pricing model scales with the total software budget under management rather than being a flat per-seat SaaS fee; reported average annual contract values are around 25,000 dollars, though actual pricing is customized per customer and not published on Sastrify's website. In a significant development, Sastrify was acquired by Deel, the global payroll and HR platform, in a deal that closed on May 5, 2026, folding Sastrify's spend management and procurement capabilities into Deel's broader finance and workforce management offering. Sastrify is used primarily by technology and software companies that have accumulated a large number of disparate SaaS subscriptions across departments and want a single system of record for contract terms, renewal dates, usage data, and vendor relationships, rather than relying on scattered spreadsheets or expense reports. Because Sastrify's core value proposition is cost visibility and negotiated savings rather than a specific piece of software functionality, its most common use case is finance and procurement teams conducting SaaS audits, consolidating duplicate tools, and building a defensible software budget ahead of renewal cycles. As a recently acquired company, prospective buyers researching Sastrify in 2026 should note that its roadmap, pricing structure, and branding are likely to evolve as it integrates into Deel's product suite, and that direct, up-to-date pricing conversations require contacting sales rather than relying on a self-serve published price list.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Sastrify is a SaaS management and procurement platform founded in Cologne, Germany in 2020 by Maximilian Messing and Sven Lackinger. It gives IT, finance, and procurement teams a centralized view of every software subscription across an organization, tracking contract terms, renewal dates, and spend.

Beyond visibility tooling, Sastrify differentiates itself with a hands-on procurement service: its team works directly with customers to benchmark pricing and negotiate better terms with software vendors, positioning the product as much as a cost-optimization service as a piece of software.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core features include a centralized SaaS inventory with automated discovery of tools in use, renewal alerts to avoid unwanted auto-renewals, spend benchmarking against comparable companies, and workflows for requesting new software purchases.

Sastrify also offers vendor negotiation support delivered by its own procurement specialists, integrations with finance and identity systems to detect shadow IT, and reporting dashboards aimed at helping finance leaders build defensible software budgets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Sastrify does not publish flat self-serve pricing; instead, it uses a custom subscription model tied to the customer's total software spend under management, with reported average annual contract values around 25,000 dollars for companies with over 100 employees.

Prospective customers need to contact Sastrify's sales team for a quote. In May 2026, Sastrify was acquired by Deel, which may affect how pricing and packaging are structured going forward as the product integrates into Deel's platform.

features.description: Automatically discovers and lists software subscriptions across an organization, reducing reliance on manual spreadsheets.

features.description: Flags upcoming contract renewals so procurement and finance teams can renegotiate or cancel before auto-renewal deadlines.

features.title: Vendor negotiation support

features.title: Spend benchmarking
features.description: Compares a company's software costs against market benchmarks to identify overpriced or redundant tools.

features.title: Shadow IT detection
features.description: Integrates with finance and identity systems to surface unauthorized or unmanaged software purchases.

features.title: Purchase request workflows
features.description: Provides structured workflows for employees to request new software, routing approvals through IT and finance.

features.title: Reporting dashboards
features.description: Gives finance leaders visibility into total software spend, trends, and savings achieved over time.

pros: Combines software visibility with hands-on vendor negotiation support, not just tracking
Reports meaningful average savings (around 30 percent) for adopting companies
Strong fit for mid-market and larger technology companies with sprawling SaaS stacks
European headquarters with European data handling may appeal to EU-based buyers
cons: No published self-serve pricing, requiring a sales conversation for even basic quotes
Pricing model tied to total software spend may be costly for companies with very high SaaS budgets
Best suited to larger organizations (100+ employees); smaller companies may find it disproportionately priced
Now part of Deel following its May 2026 acquisition, which may bring product and pricing changes

faqs.question: What does Sastrify do?
faqs.answer: Sastrify is a SaaS management and procurement platform that gives finance, IT, and procurement teams visibility into software subscriptions, tracks renewals, and helps negotiate better vendor pricing.

faqs.question: How much does Sastrify cost?
faqs.answer: Sastrify uses custom pricing based on a company's total software spend under management rather than a flat published price; average annual contract values have been reported around 25,000 dollars.

faqs.answer: Sastrify was founded in 2020 by Maximilian Messing and Sven Lackinger.

faqs.question: Where is Sastrify headquartered?
faqs.answer: Sastrify is headquartered in Cologne, Germany.

faqs.question: Was Sastrify acquired?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sastrify was acquired by Deel, the global payroll and HR platform, in a deal that closed on May 5, 2026.

faqs.question: Who is Sastrify designed for?
faqs.answer: Sastrify primarily serves mid-market and larger technology companies, especially those with more than 100 employees and significant SaaS spend.

faqs.question: Does Sastrify help negotiate software contracts?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sastrify's procurement team works directly with customers to benchmark and negotiate pricing with software vendors on their behalf.

faqs.question: Is Sastrify only for IT teams?
faqs.answer: No, Sastrify is used by IT, finance, and procurement teams jointly, since SaaS spend management typically spans all three functions.

enrichment.short_description: Sauce Labs is a cloud-based continuous testing platform that lets development and QA teams run automated and manual tests for web and mobile apps across real and virtual browsers and devices.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered monthly or annual subscription based on concurrency, plus custom Enterprise pricing
enrichment.starting_price: $39 per month (Live Testing plan, billed annually)
enrichment.seo_title: Sauce Labs Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare Sauce Labs pricing plans, features, and company details for 2026, including verified facts on founding, headquarters, and testing capabilities.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Sauce Labs is a cloud-based continuous testing platform founded in 2008, co-founded in part by the original creator of the Selenium browser automation framework, and headquartered at 450 Sansome Street in San Francisco, California, with roughly 300 to 320 employees as of 2026. The company provides infrastructure for running automated and manual tests of web and mobile applications across thousands of real and virtual browser and device combinations in the cloud, removing the need for engineering teams to maintain their own device labs or browser farms. Its platform is organized around three core self-serve products: Live Testing for manual, interactive cross-browser and mobile testing starting at 39 dollars per month billed annually (49 dollars month-to-month); Virtual Cloud, which adds automated testing across desktop browsers and mobile emulators and simulators along with 500 monthly visual testing snapshots, starting at 149 dollars per month billed annually (199 dollars monthly); and Real Device Cloud, which extends automated and manual testing to thousands of physical mobile devices, starting at 199 dollars per month billed annually (249 dollars monthly). All three self-serve plans include unlimited users and unlimited testing minutes, with pricing instead scaling by the number of parallel (concurrent) test sessions a team needs. Sauce Labs also sells Sauce Mobile App Distribution, a beta-testing and app distribution product priced at 249 dollars per month billed annually and including 20 unique apps and 100 beta testers, as well as standalone visual testing and error reporting products. For larger organizations, Sauce Labs offers custom Enterprise pricing that adds single sign-on, unified analytics across testing products, private device cloud options, premium support, and professional services. The company reported roughly 92 million dollars in annual recurring revenue as of 2024 figures cited by industry trackers, and has raised over 150 million dollars in funding over its history, reflecting its position as one of the longest-running and most established players in the cloud testing and quality engineering space, competing with tools like BrowserStack. Sauce Labs is most commonly used by software engineering and QA teams that need to validate cross-browser and cross-device compatibility as part of continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines, and its integrations with popular CI/CD tools and test frameworks like Selenium, Appium, and Cypress make it a common choice for teams already invested in open-source test automation tooling who want managed infrastructure rather than self-hosted device grids.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Sauce Labs is a cloud-based continuous testing platform founded in 2008 and headquartered in San Francisco, California. It was co-founded in part by the original creator of Selenium, the widely used open-source browser automation framework, and has grown into one of the most established providers of cloud-hosted testing infrastructure for web and mobile applications.

The platform lets engineering and QA teams run automated and manual tests across thousands of real and virtual browser and device combinations without maintaining their own hardware labs, integrating with popular open-source frameworks like Selenium, Appium, and Cypress as well as CI/CD pipelines.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Sauce Labs offers a Virtual Cloud for automated and manual testing across desktop browsers and mobile emulators/simulators, and a Real Device Cloud for testing on thousands of physical mobile devices. Live Testing supports manual, interactive cross-browser and mobile testing sessions.

Additional capabilities include visual testing snapshots for catching UI regressions, error reporting for surfacing frontend issues, and Sauce Mobile App Distribution for beta testing and distributing mobile builds to testers before release.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Sauce Labs prices its three core self-serve plans by concurrency (parallel test sessions) rather than by seats: Live Testing starts at 39 dollars per month, Virtual Cloud at 149 dollars per month, and Real Device Cloud at 199 dollars per month, all billed annually with higher month-to-month rates. All plans include unlimited users and unlimited testing minutes.

Enterprise pricing is custom and adds SSO, unified analytics, private device cloud, premium support, and professional services for larger organizations with more complex compliance or scale requirements.

features.title: Real Device Cloud
features.description: Provides access to thousands of physical mobile devices in the cloud for automated and manual testing without maintaining an in-house device lab.

features.title: Virtual Cloud (emulators and simulators)
features.description: Runs automated and manual tests across desktop browsers and mobile emulators/simulators at scale.

features.title: Live interactive testing
features.description: Allows manual, real-time cross-browser and mobile testing sessions for exploratory QA work.

features.title: Visual testing
features.description: Captures visual snapshots to detect unintended UI regressions across builds, included in Virtual and Real Device Cloud plans.

features.title: CI/CD and framework integrations
features.description: Integrates with Selenium, Appium, Cypress, and common CI/CD tools to run tests as part of automated pipelines.

features.title: Error reporting
features.description: Surfaces and aggregates frontend errors to help teams diagnose issues found during testing.

features.title: Mobile app distribution
features.description: Sauce Mobile App Distribution lets teams distribute beta builds to testers and manage feedback before release.

features.title: Enterprise-grade security and analytics
features.description: Enterprise plans add SSO, unified analytics dashboards, and private device cloud options for larger, compliance-driven organizations.

pros: Long track record since 2008 with deep roots in the Selenium open-source ecosystem
Unlimited users and unlimited testing minutes included even on entry-level plans
Combines real device, virtual/emulator, visual, and error-reporting testing under one platform
Strong CI/CD and open-source framework integrations for automation-heavy teams
cons: Pricing scales by concurrency, so teams needing many parallel tests can face steep costs
Entry-level plans are limited to a single parallel test session
Enterprise features like SSO and private device cloud require custom pricing and sales contact
Multiple add-on products (visual testing, error reporting, app distribution) can complicate the overall pricing picture

faqs.question: How much does Sauce Labs cost?
faqs.answer: Sauce Labs starts at 39 dollars per month for Live Testing (billed annually), with Virtual Cloud at 149 dollars per month and Real Device Cloud at 199 dollars per month; Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: When was Sauce Labs founded?
faqs.answer: Sauce Labs was founded in 2008 and was co-founded in part by the original creator of the Selenium testing framework.

faqs.question: Where is Sauce Labs headquartered?
faqs.answer: Sauce Labs is headquartered in San Francisco, California, at 450 Sansome Street.

faqs.question: Does Sauce Labs support real device testing?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Real Device Cloud plan provides access to thousands of physical mobile devices for automated and manual testing.

faqs.question: What frameworks does Sauce Labs integrate with?
faqs.answer: Sauce Labs integrates with open-source frameworks like Selenium, Appium, and Cypress, as well as common CI/CD pipelines.

faqs.question: Does Sauce Labs offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sauce Labs offers a free trial in addition to its paid Live Testing, Virtual Cloud, and Real Device Cloud plans.

faqs.question: Are Sauce Labs plans limited by number of users?
faqs.answer: No, all three self-serve plans include unlimited users; pricing instead scales based on the number of parallel test sessions.

faqs.question: Does Sauce Labs offer visual testing?
faqs.answer: Yes, Virtual Cloud and Real Device Cloud plans include 500 visual testing snapshots per month, with a dedicated Sauce Visual product also available.

use_cases.title: Cross-browser compatibility testing
use_cases.description: QA and engineering teams validate that web applications work correctly across many browser and OS combinations without maintaining local test environments.

use_cases.title: Mobile app testing on real devices
use_cases.description: Mobile teams test app builds on physical devices in the cloud to catch device-specific bugs before release.

use_cases.title: CI/CD test automation at scale
use_cases.description: Engineering teams integrate Sauce Labs into CI/CD pipelines to run parallel automated test suites on every code change.

features: Manual cross-browser and mobile app testing
Automated and manual desktop browser and emulator/simulator testing
Testing on thousands of real mobile devices

enrichment.short_description: Savio is a customer feedback management tool that centralizes feature requests from support, sales, and success tools like Intercom, HubSpot, and Slack so B2B SaaS teams can build evidence-based product roadmaps.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered per-user subscription with unlimited free viewer seats
enrichment.seo_title: Savio Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See Savio's verified 2026 pricing plans, features, pros and cons, and how its customer feedback and roadmapping tool works for B2B SaaS teams.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Savio is a customer feedback management platform founded in 2019 by Kareem Mayan and Ryan Stocker and headquartered in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Canada, operating as a small, lean team of roughly one to ten people rather than a large venture-scale company. The product is designed for B2B SaaS teams to centralize product feedback and feature requests coming in from disparate channels, integrating with tools such as Intercom, HubSpot, Slack, Help Scout, Zendesk, JIRA, Shortcut, Salesforce, and Segment so that support, sales, and customer success teams can log requests directly from where they already work. Savio's core workflow lets teams triage incoming feedback, tag and deduplicate similar requests, attach requests to specific customer accounts and revenue data, and build public or private voting boards where customers can upvote features they want. This customer-linked feedback data is then used to build prioritized, evidence-based product roadmaps, helping product managers justify what to build next based on actual revenue-weighted demand rather than the loudest anecdote. Savio uses a tiered per-user pricing model with unlimited free viewer seats, starting with the Essential plan at 39 dollars per month billed annually (which includes one paid user, a single roadmap, and core integrations with Intercom, Help Scout, and Slack), moving up to the Professional plan at 79 dollars per month billed annually (adding customer segmentation, five roadmaps, product areas, a Chrome extension, and integrations with Zendesk, Zapier, JIRA, and Shortcut), and the Business plan at 249 dollars per month billed annually (adding multiple products, unlimited roadmaps, Segment.com, HubSpot, and Salesforce CRM integrations, a custom SLA, and priority support). An Enterprise tier with custom terms, invoicing, and a custom Data Processing Agreement is available for larger buyers. All plans include a free 14-day trial with no credit card required, and additional paid users can be added to any plan for a per-user fee that increases with the plan tier. Because Savio is a small, founder-led company rather than a large enterprise vendor, it tends to appeal to early- and growth-stage B2B SaaS companies that want a focused, affordable feedback and roadmap tool without the complexity or cost of larger product management suites, and its close integrations with common support and CRM tools make it easy to adopt without disrupting existing team workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Savio is a customer feedback management platform founded in 2019 by Kareem Mayan and Ryan Stocker, headquartered in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Canada. It is built specifically for B2B SaaS teams that need to collect, organize, and prioritize feature requests coming in from support, sales, and success conversations.

Rather than being a broad product management suite, Savio focuses narrowly on the feedback-to-roadmap workflow, integrating tightly with tools teams already use like Intercom, HubSpot, Slack, and JIRA so that feedback capture requires minimal extra effort from customer-facing teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Savio lets teams log and triage feedback directly from Intercom, Help Scout, Slack, Zendesk, and other tools, tagging and deduplicating similar requests as they come in. Feedback can be linked to specific customer accounts and revenue data to support evidence-based prioritization.

The platform also includes public or private voting boards for gathering direct customer input, product roadmaps for communicating priorities, and integrations with CRM and issue-tracking tools like HubSpot, Salesforce, and JIRA to close the loop once features ship.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Savio offers four tiers: Essential at 39 dollars per month, Professional at 79 dollars per month, Business at 249 dollars per month (all billed annually, with monthly billing also available), and a custom-priced Enterprise tier. Each plan includes one paid user with unlimited free viewer seats, and additional paid users can be added for a per-plan fee.
Higher tiers unlock more roadmaps, customer segmentation, additional integrations, and priority support. All plans include a 14-day free trial that does not require a credit card.

features.title: Multi-channel feedback capture
features.description: Logs feedback directly from Intercom, Help Scout, Slack, Zendesk, and email so support and sales teams can submit requests without leaving their workflow.

features.title: Feedback triage and deduplication
features.description: Lets teams tag, merge, and organize similar feature requests into a single centralized backlog.

features.title: Customer and revenue-linked prioritization
features.description: Attaches feedback to specific customer accounts and revenue data to support evidence-based roadmap decisions.

features.title: Voting boards
features.description: Public or private boards where customers can submit and upvote feature requests directly.

features.title: Product roadmaps
features.description: Visual roadmaps to communicate what is planned, in progress, and shipped, closing the loop with customers who requested features.

features.title: CRM and issue tracker integrations
features.description: Connects with HubSpot, Salesforce, JIRA, and Shortcut so feedback and engineering work stay linked.

features.title: Customer segmentation
features.description: Available on Professional and higher plans, letting teams filter feedback by customer segment or account tier.

features.title: Chrome extension
features.description: Lets team members log feedback from any web page, available on Professional and higher plans.

pros: Focused specifically on B2B SaaS feedback-to-roadmap workflows rather than being a broad suite
Unlimited free viewer seats keep costs down for teams that mostly need to browse feedback
Strong integrations with common support tools like Intercom, Help Scout, and Slack
14-day free trial with no credit card required lowers the barrier to evaluate the product
cons: Small, lean team size may mean a slower feature release cadence than larger, VC-backed competitors
Entry-level Essential plan is limited to a single roadmap
Higher-value integrations like Salesforce and Segment are gated behind the more expensive Business plan
Per-user pricing for additional paid seats can add up for larger cross-functional teams

faqs.question: What does Savio do?
faqs.answer: Savio centralizes customer feedback and feature requests from support, sales, and success tools so B2B SaaS teams can build evidence-based, prioritized product roadmaps.

faqs.question: How much does Savio cost?
faqs.answer: Savio starts at 39 dollars per month for the Essential plan billed annually, with Professional at 79 dollars per month and Business at 249 dollars per month; Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: When was Savio founded?
faqs.answer: Savio was founded in 2019 by Kareem Mayan and Ryan Stocker.

faqs.question: Where is Savio based?
faqs.answer: Savio is headquartered in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Canada.

faqs.question: Does Savio offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Savio plans include a 14-day free trial and do not require a credit card to start.

faqs.question: What integrations does Savio support?
faqs.answer: Savio integrates with Intercom, Help Scout, Slack, Zendesk, JIRA, Shortcut, HubSpot, Salesforce, Zapier, and Segment, depending on the plan.

faqs.question: Are viewer seats free in Savio?
faqs.answer: Yes, Savio includes unlimited free viewer seats on every plan; only active/paid users are billed.

faqs.question: Is Savio suited for enterprise companies?
faqs.answer: Savio offers a custom Enterprise tier with custom terms and invoicing, but the product is most commonly used by early- and growth-stage B2B SaaS companies.

enrichment.short_description: SavvyCal is a scheduling and calendar booking tool that lets people share availability, overlay their calendar on top of an invitee's, and offer flexible booking links, team scheduling, and paid bookings.
enrichment.company_size: Small team of approximately 4-6 employees (bootstrapped, TinySeed-backed)
enrichment.seo_title: SavvyCal Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Explore SavvyCal's verified 2026 pricing, features, and company details, including how its calendar scheduling tool compares for teams and individuals.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SavvyCal is a scheduling and calendar booking tool founded by Derrick Reimer, who began building the product in March 2020 and onboarded its first customers that summer, with the company backed by a small amount of funding from the startup accelerator TinySeed. SavvyCal is headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota, and operates as a small, largely remote and bootstrapped team of roughly four to six people, a notably lean headcount compared to venture-scale scheduling competitors like Calendly. Reimer previously co-founded the email marketing platform Drip, which was acquired by Leadpages in 2016, and Codetree, a GitHub issue management tool acquired the same year, giving SavvyCal an experienced solo-founder-led pedigree. SavvyCal's key differentiator is its calendar overlay feature, which lets an invitee see their own calendar layered directly on top of the host's availability when picking a meeting time, reducing the back-and-forth of choosing a time that does not conflict with the invitee's existing commitments. The product supports unlimited scheduling links and calendar connections on every paid plan, along with collective, round robin, and group scheduling modes for teams, meeting polls, availability sharing, website embedding, a browser extension, automated reminders and workflows, and integrations with common conferencing tools, CRMs, and Zapier, plus API and webhook access. SavvyCal's pricing is straightforward and per-user: the Basic plan costs 10 dollars per user per month and includes unlimited calendars, unlimited scheduling links, and team scheduling; the Premium plan costs 17 dollars per user per month and adds custom domains, the ability to delegate scheduling to an assistant, and paid bookings so hosts can charge for meetings directly through their booking page. Annual billing on both plans saves customers roughly two months of cost compared to paying monthly. SavvyCal does not offer a permanently free tier, relying instead on a free trial paired with a 30-day money-back guarantee to let prospective customers evaluate the product risk-free. As of 2024, SavvyCal's estimated annual recurring revenue was reported around 1 million dollars with an estimated company valuation of 3 million dollars, figures consistent with an intentionally small, profitable, founder-led SaaS business rather than a venture-funded growth-at-all-costs company. SavvyCal is most commonly chosen by independent professionals, consultants, sales teams, and recruiters who schedule a high volume of external meetings and want a more considerate booking experience for invitees than typical scheduling tools provide, as well as by teams that want round robin or collective scheduling for sales and customer success workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: SavvyCal is a calendar scheduling tool founded by Derrick Reimer, who started building the product in March 2020, headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. The company is intentionally small, operating with roughly four to six team members and backed by modest funding from the startup accelerator TinySeed rather than traditional venture capital.

SavvyCal's signature feature is letting invitees overlay their own calendar on top of the host's availability when booking a meeting, which is designed to reduce scheduling conflicts and make the booking experience feel more considerate than typical link-and-pick scheduling tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The product supports unlimited scheduling links, unlimited calendar connections, and multiple scheduling modes including collective, round robin, and group scheduling for teams. It also includes meeting polls, availability sharing, website embedding, and a browser extension.

Additional capabilities include automated reminders and workflows, conferencing and CRM integrations, Zapier support, API and webhook access, and on the Premium plan, custom domains, delegated scheduling for assistants, and the ability to charge for paid bookings.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SavvyCal offers two paid plans billed per user: Basic at 10 dollars per user per month with unlimited calendars, scheduling links, and team scheduling, and Premium at 17 dollars per user per month adding custom domains, delegated scheduling, and paid bookings.

There is no permanent free plan; instead, SavvyCal offers a free trial backed by a 30-day money-back guarantee. Annual billing on either plan saves roughly two months of cost compared to paying monthly.

Small, founder-led team suggests fast, direct customer support

cons: No permanent free plan, only a trial and money-back guarantee
Small team size may mean a slower pace of new feature development than larger competitors like Calendly
Paid bookings and custom domains require the more expensive Premium tier
Less brand recognition than category leaders, which may affect invitee trust for cold outreach

faqs.answer: SavvyCal costs 10 dollars per user per month for the Basic plan and 17 dollars per user per month for the Premium plan, with roughly two months free on annual billing.

faqs.answer: SavvyCal was founded by Derrick Reimer, who began building the product in March 2020.

faqs.question: Where is SavvyCal based?
faqs.answer: SavvyCal is headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

faqs.answer: Yes, both plans support collective, round robin, and group scheduling for teams.

faqs.answer: Yes, the Premium plan includes paid bookings, letting hosts charge invitees directly through their scheduling page.

faqs.answer: SavvyCal operates as a small, largely bootstrapped team of roughly four to six people.

enrichment.short_description: Scalar is an API platform that renders interactive, OpenAPI-based API reference documentation and pairs it with an open-source REST API client, an API registry, and SDK generation tools for developer portals.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium tiered subscription plus custom Enterprise pricing
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; Pro plan starts at $72 per month
enrichment.seo_title: Scalar Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See Scalar's verified 2026 pricing plans for API documentation, its open-source REST client, and how it compares for developer teams.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Scalar is an API platform founded in 2023 by CEO Marc Laventure and co-founder Cameron Rohani, headquartered in San Francisco, California, and run by a small team of around ten engineers and designers. The company describes itself as building on top of the open-source Scalar project, a widely adopted renderer for OpenAPI and Swagger specifications that developers use to generate modern, interactive API reference documentation; the open-source project is hosted publicly on GitHub and includes a standalone REST API client alongside the documentation renderer. Commercially, Scalar layers a hosted platform on top of that open-source foundation, letting teams hand it a YAML or JSON OpenAPI specification and receive parsed, rendered documentation with an interactive request runner wired up against the team's live API, effectively combining reference docs with a built-in API testing tool. The Free plan costs nothing and includes hosted OpenAPI documentation, a built-in API client with collections and environment management, one editor seat, unlimited viewer seats, and 50 monthly credits for Scalar's AI agent feature (branded Agent Scalar), though it is capped at three APIs in the registry. The Pro plan costs 72 dollars per month and adds custom domains and subdomains, Git-based sync, Markdown and MDX support, generated SDKs in languages such as TypeScript, Python, C#, Java, PHP, and Go (each additional SDK language costs 100 dollars extra), hosted MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, automated GitHub workflows, full developer portals with landing pages and guides, API versioning, and 500 monthly Agent Scalar credits. The Enterprise plan is custom-priced and adds SSO/SAML, role-based access control, priority support with SLAs, dedicated Slack or Teams channels, migration services for SDKs and MCP servers, and custom Agent Scalar credit allocations. Because Scalar ties its commercial platform to the popular open-source renderer, it is frequently adopted by API-first companies and developer tools startups that already use or are evaluating the open-source Scalar client and want a hosted, brandable developer portal without building documentation infrastructure themselves, as well as teams that want to expose their APIs to AI coding agents via hosted MCP servers, reflecting the company's positioning around the growing overlap between API tooling and AI agent development in 2026.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Scalar is an API platform founded in 2023 by Marc Laventure and Cameron Rohani, headquartered in San Francisco and run by a small team of around ten people. It is built around the open-source Scalar project, a popular renderer for OpenAPI and Swagger specifications that also ships a standalone interactive REST API client.

The commercial Scalar platform layers hosted documentation, an API registry, SDK generation, and developer portal tooling on top of that open-source foundation, letting teams turn an OpenAPI specification into a full branded developer portal with a live, interactive request runner.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Scalar parses OpenAPI/Swagger specs and renders interactive documentation with a built-in request runner that lets developers test API calls directly from the docs. Its open-source REST client supports collections, environments, and pre/post-request scripting.

Paid plans add custom domains, Git sync for docs-as-code workflows, Markdown/MDX authoring, generated SDKs in multiple languages, hosted MCP servers for AI agent integration, API versioning, and full developer portal features like landing pages and guides.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The Free plan includes hosted docs, the API client, one editor seat, unlimited viewers, and 50 monthly AI credits, limited to three APIs in the registry. The Pro plan costs 72 dollars per month and adds custom domains, Git sync, SDK generation, hosted MCP servers, and 500 monthly AI credits.
Enterprise pricing is custom and adds SSO/SAML, RBAC, SLA-backed priority support, and migration services. Additional SDK languages on the Pro plan cost 100 dollars each per month.

features.description: Renders API reference docs directly from an OpenAPI/Swagger specification, including an interactive request runner.

features.description: Lets teams expose their API to AI coding agents via a hosted Model Context Protocol server.

features.title: API registry
features.description: Tracks and organizes multiple API specifications; the Free plan is capped at three APIs.

features.description: Pro and Enterprise plans support custom domains, landing pages, and guides for a fully branded developer portal.

features.title: Enterprise security and support
features.description: Enterprise plan adds SSO/SAML, role-based access control, and SLA-backed priority support.

pros: Built on a widely adopted open-source project, giving credibility and a large existing user base
Free plan includes a genuinely useful API client and hosted docs, not just a stripped-down demo
Combines documentation, API client, and SDK generation in a single platform
Early mover on hosted MCP servers for AI agent integration with APIs
cons: Small team (around 10 people) as of 2023 founding may limit support responsiveness at scale
Free plan is capped at just 3 APIs in the registry
Additional SDK languages on the Pro plan cost extra (100 dollars each per month)
Enterprise features like SSO require custom pricing and a sales conversation

faqs.question: What is Scalar used for?
faqs.answer: Scalar is used to generate interactive API reference documentation from OpenAPI/Swagger specs, paired with an API client, SDK generation, and developer portal tooling.

faqs.question: How much does Scalar cost?
faqs.answer: Scalar has a free plan, a Pro plan at 72 dollars per month, and custom Enterprise pricing.

faqs.question: Who founded Scalar and when?
faqs.answer: Scalar was founded in 2023 by Marc Laventure (CEO) and Cameron Rohani.

faqs.question: Is Scalar open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Scalar renderer and API client are open source and available on GitHub; the hosted platform adds commercial features on top.

faqs.question: Where is Scalar headquartered?
faqs.answer: Scalar is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

faqs.question: Does Scalar support generating SDKs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pro plan can generate client SDKs in languages including TypeScript, Python, C#, Java, PHP, and Go.

faqs.question: What is Agent Scalar?
faqs.answer: Agent Scalar is Scalar's AI agent feature, included as a monthly credit allowance on both the Free and Pro plans.

faqs.question: Can Scalar expose APIs to AI coding agents?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pro plan includes hosted MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that let AI agents interact with a team's API.

enrichment.short_description: Scalekit is a B2B authentication infrastructure platform providing enterprise SSO, SCIM provisioning, and passwordless login for SaaS applications, along with an authentication and actions layer built for AI agent applications.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium, usage-based (tool calls/API usage) plus custom Enterprise pricing
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; Growth plan starts at $99 per month
enrichment.company_size: Small team; exact headcount not publicly disclosed (seed-stage startup)
enrichment.headquarters: United States (Delaware-registered), with an engineering office in Bengaluru, India
enrichment.seo_title: Scalekit Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See Scalekit's verified 2026 pricing plans for SSO, SCIM, and AI agent authentication, plus company background and key features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Scalekit is a B2B authentication infrastructure company founded in 2023 by Satya Padmanabham Devarakonda (CEO) and Ravi Krishna Kishore Madabhushi, registered in the United States (Delaware) with an engineering office operated through Scalekit Technologies India Private Limited in Bengaluru, India. The company raised a 5.5 million dollar seed round led by Together Fund and Z47, with angel investors including Adam Frankl, Oliver Jay, and Jagadeesh Kunda, and operates as a small, seed-stage team without a publicly disclosed exact headcount. Scalekit's core product helps B2B SaaS companies add enterprise-grade authentication features, including SAML and OIDC single sign-on, SCIM user provisioning, passkeys, magic links, social login, and one-time-passcode authentication, integrating with identity providers such as Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, OneLogin, and JumpCloud, and offering automatic certificate rotation so engineering teams don't have to build and maintain this infrastructure themselves. In 2026 the company has positioned itself heavily around a newer use case: authentication and authorization for AI agents, describing its platform as the 'auth stack for AI apps' that unifies human, AI agent, and API authentication in a single system, letting agentic applications authenticate incoming agents and securely connect to external tools on a user's behalf. Scalekit's AgentKit product is priced on a freemium, usage-based model tied to monthly 'tool calls': the Free plan includes 5,000 tool calls per month along with unlimited connected accounts, access to more than 500 pre-built connectors, the ability to bring your own API credentials, community and email support, and 30-day audit log retention, automatically prompting an upgrade to Growth once the free limit is exceeded. The Growth plan costs 99 dollars per month and includes 100,000 tool calls with overage billed at 0.50 dollars per additional 1,000 calls, adding custom connectors, an API proxy, white-label branding, private Slack support, unlimited development and staging environments, coding agent plugins, and an optional EU data residency add-on for an additional 99 dollars per month. The Enterprise plan is custom-priced with negotiated volume and rates, and adds VPC or on-premises deployment, a 99.99 percent uptime SLA, a forward-deployed engineer plus dedicated customer success manager, SIEM integrations, and audit log retention extendable up to one year. Scalekit is most relevant to B2B SaaS engineering teams that need to ship enterprise SSO and provisioning quickly to close enterprise deals, as well as newer AI-native companies building agentic products that need a way to authenticate and authorize AI agents acting on behalf of users across external tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Scalekit is a B2B authentication infrastructure company founded in 2023 by Satya Padmanabham Devarakonda and Ravi Krishna Kishore Madabhushi, registered in the United States with an engineering office in Bengaluru, India. The company raised a 5.5 million dollar seed round led by Together Fund and Z47.

Scalekit's original focus is helping B2B SaaS companies add enterprise SSO, SCIM provisioning, and passwordless authentication without building that infrastructure in-house. In 2026 it has expanded into authentication and authorization for AI agent applications, positioning itself as a unified auth layer for human, agent, and API access.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform supports SAML and OIDC single sign-on, SCIM user provisioning, passkeys, magic links, social login, and OTP authentication, integrating with identity providers like Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, OneLogin, and JumpCloud with automatic certificate rotation.

For AI agent use cases, Scalekit's AgentKit product offers unlimited connected accounts, over 500 pre-built connectors, an API proxy, custom connectors, webhooks, and coding agent plugins, letting agentic applications securely authenticate and act on behalf of users across external tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Scalekit's AgentKit pricing is usage-based by monthly tool calls: the Free plan includes 5,000 tool calls per month, and the Growth plan costs 99 dollars per month for 100,000 tool calls with overage at 0.50 dollars per 1,000 additional calls.

The Enterprise plan is custom-priced with negotiated volume rates and adds VPC/on-prem deployment, a 99.99 percent uptime SLA, dedicated engineering and customer success support, and SIEM integrations.

features.description: Supports passkeys, magic links, and one-time passcodes as alternatives to traditional password logins.

features.description: AgentKit ships with over 500 pre-built connectors for linking AI agents to external tools and services.

features.description: Handles SSO certificate rotation automatically across supported identity providers, reducing maintenance overhead.

features.description: Growth plan adds an API proxy and the ability to build custom connectors for bespoke integrations.

features.description: Tracks authentication and access events with configurable retention, extendable up to one year on Enterprise.

features.description: Enterprise plan supports deploying Scalekit within a customer's own VPC or on-premises environment.

pros: Combines traditional B2B SSO/SCIM infrastructure with newer AI agent authentication in one platform
Free plan offers a meaningful 5,000 monthly tool calls with unlimited connected accounts
Broad identity provider support (Okta, Entra ID, OneLogin, JumpCloud) with automatic certificate rotation
Backed by credible investors (Together Fund, Z47) suggesting continued product investment
cons: Seed-stage startup with an undisclosed small team, which may raise longevity questions for enterprise buyers
Company location is somewhat ambiguous (Delaware registration with India-based engineering office)
Overage charges on the Growth plan can add unpredictable costs for high-usage AI agent applications
EU data residency is a paid add-on rather than included by default

faqs.question: What does Scalekit do?
faqs.answer: Scalekit provides B2B authentication infrastructure, including enterprise SSO, SCIM provisioning, and passwordless login, plus an authentication layer for AI agent applications.

faqs.question: How much does Scalekit cost?
faqs.answer: Scalekit's AgentKit has a free plan with 5,000 monthly tool calls, a Growth plan at 99 dollars per month for 100,000 tool calls, and custom Enterprise pricing.

faqs.question: When was Scalekit founded?
faqs.answer: Scalekit was founded in 2023 by Satya Padmanabham Devarakonda and Ravi Krishna Kishore Madabhushi.

faqs.question: Where is Scalekit based?
faqs.answer: Scalekit is registered in the United States (Delaware) and operates an engineering office in Bengaluru, India.

faqs.question: Does Scalekit support AI agent authentication?
faqs.answer: Yes, its AgentKit product provides authentication and authorization specifically for AI agent applications, including connecting agents to external tools.

faqs.question: What identity providers does Scalekit integrate with?
faqs.answer: Scalekit integrates with Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, OneLogin, JumpCloud, and other SAML/OIDC-compatible identity providers.

faqs.question: Has Scalekit raised funding?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scalekit raised a 5.5 million dollar seed round led by Together Fund and Z47.

faqs.question: Does Scalekit offer on-premises deployment?
faqs.answer: Yes, VPC and on-premises deployment is available on the Enterprise plan.

enrichment.short_description: Scalenut is an AI-powered SEO and content platform that combines keyword research, AI content creation and optimization, content audits, and generative-engine-optimization (GEO) tracking across AI answer engines like ChatGPT and Google AI Overviews.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered subscription, billed monthly or annually with promotional discounts
enrichment.starting_price: $24 per month (Starter plan, promotional pricing; regular list price $59 per month)
enrichment.company_size: Approximately 30-80 employees (estimates vary by source and date)
enrichment.seo_title: Scalenut Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare Scalenut's verified 2026 pricing plans, AI content and SEO features, and how its generative-engine-optimization tools work.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Scalenut is an AI-powered SEO and content platform founded on August 20, 2020 by Gaurav Goyal, Mayank Jain, and Saurabh Wadhawan, and headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana, India, operating under the legal entity Scalenut Technologies Private Limited. Employee counts reported across business data providers vary by date, ranging from roughly 28 to over 80 people, with one source citing a team of about 60 people generating over 10 million dollars in revenue in 2024, suggesting a mid-sized, profitable company rather than an early-stage startup. Scalenut's platform is built around helping content teams plan, create, optimize, and analyze content for search rankings, combining traditional SEO tooling such as keyword clustering and content audits with newer generative-engine-optimization (GEO) capabilities that track how a brand's content performs and is cited within AI answer engines like ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity. The company markets itself as running a swarm of specialized AI agents alongside a team of human GEO experts who work continuously on a customer's content strategy. As of mid-2026, Scalenut runs limited-time promotional pricing offering 60 percent off its standard rates: the Starter plan is 24 dollars per month (down from a regular price of 59 dollars per month) and includes tracking of 10 AI prompts weekly across ChatGPT and Google AI Overviews, one workspace, the ability to create or optimize 10 articles per month, and 10 keyword clusters per month. The Plus plan is 36 dollars per month (down from 89 dollars per month) and adds tracking of 25 weekly prompts, two workspaces, 60 articles per month, 60 keyword clusters per month, content audits covering up to 400 pages per month, up to four team members, and features like advanced content audits, internal linking suggestions, and auto-publishing. The Professional plan is 80 dollars per month (down from 199 dollars per month) and adds tracking of 100 weekly prompts across ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity, unlimited workspaces, 150 articles per month, 150 keyword clusters per month, content audits covering up to 2,000 pages per month, unlimited team members, and a dedicated customer success manager with priority support. Scalenut also offers a custom-priced VIP Service tier in which dedicated GEO experts and AI agents handle research, content development, backlink building, technical audits, and weekly reporting on the customer's behalf, positioning it as a hybrid software-plus-managed-service offering. Because Scalenut explicitly tracks and optimizes for citations within AI-generated answers in addition to traditional organic search rankings, it has positioned itself for the shift toward AI-driven search discovery, making it a relevant choice for content and SEO teams that want visibility into both classic Google rankings and how often their brand is surfaced by AI chat and answer engines.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Scalenut is an AI-powered SEO and content platform founded in August 2020 by Gaurav Goyal, Mayank Jain, and Saurabh Wadhawan, headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana, India. The company has grown into a mid-sized, reportedly profitable business, with revenue estimates around 10 million dollars in 2024.

The platform combines traditional SEO capabilities like keyword research and content audits with AI-driven content generation and, more recently, generative-engine-optimization (GEO) tools that track how content performs within AI answer engines such as ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Scalenut's core toolkit includes AI-assisted content creation and optimization, keyword clustering for topical planning, content audits that scan existing pages for improvement opportunities, and internal linking suggestions.

Its newer GEO tracking features monitor how frequently and favorably a brand appears within AI-generated answers across ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity, extending SEO tracking beyond traditional search engine result pages.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: As of mid-2026, Scalenut runs a 60 percent promotional discount across its self-serve plans: Starter at 24 dollars per month, Plus at 36 dollars per month, and Professional at 80 dollars per month, all discounted from higher regular list prices. Each tier increases limits on tracked AI prompts, articles created, keyword clusters, workspaces, and team members.

A custom-priced VIP Service tier adds dedicated human GEO experts working alongside AI agents to handle content strategy, backlinks, and technical audits on the customer's behalf as a managed service.

features.title: AI content creation and optimization
features.description: Generates and optimizes articles using AI, with guidance based on top-ranking competitor content for a given keyword.

features.title: Keyword clustering
features.description: Groups related keywords into topical clusters to guide a more structured content strategy.

features.title: Content audits
features.description: Scans existing published content (up to 2,000 pages per month on the Professional plan) to identify optimization opportunities.

features.title: Generative-engine-optimization (GEO) tracking
features.description: Tracks how often and how favorably a brand's content is cited within AI answer engines like ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity.

features.title: Internal linking suggestions
features.description: Recommends internal links to improve site structure and topical authority, available on Plus and higher plans.

features.title: Auto-publish workflows
features.description: Allows optimized content to be automatically published to a connected CMS, available on Plus and higher plans.

features.title: Multi-workspace support
features.description: Professional plan supports unlimited workspaces for managing multiple domains or brands from one account.

features.title: VIP managed service
features.description: Custom-priced tier pairing dedicated human GEO experts with AI agents for hands-off content strategy execution.

pros: Combines traditional SEO tooling with newer AI-answer-engine (GEO) tracking in one platform
Tiered pricing scales cleanly from solo creators to agencies with unlimited workspaces on Professional
Managed VIP Service option available for teams that want a hands-off, expert-led approach
Reportedly profitable, mid-sized company with a multi-year track record since 2020
cons: Advertised starting prices are promotional and discounted from higher regular list prices, which may not be permanent
Lower tiers cap workspaces, prompt tracking, and content audit volume fairly tightly
Employee count estimates vary significantly across data sources, making company size hard to pin down precisely
GEO/AI-answer-engine tracking is a newer capability with less of a proven track record than established SEO metrics

faqs.question: What does Scalenut do?
faqs.answer: Scalenut is an AI content and SEO platform that helps teams research keywords, create and optimize content, audit existing pages, and track visibility in both traditional search and AI answer engines.

faqs.question: How much does Scalenut cost?
faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, Scalenut's promotional pricing starts at 24 dollars per month for Starter, 36 dollars per month for Plus, and 80 dollars per month for Professional, with a custom-priced VIP Service tier also available.

faqs.question: When was Scalenut founded?
faqs.answer: Scalenut was founded on August 20, 2020 by Gaurav Goyal, Mayank Jain, and Saurabh Wadhawan.

faqs.question: Where is Scalenut headquartered?
faqs.answer: Scalenut is headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana, India.

faqs.question: What is GEO tracking in Scalenut?
faqs.answer: GEO (generative-engine-optimization) tracking monitors how often and how favorably a brand's content is cited within AI answer engines like ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity.

faqs.question: Does Scalenut support multiple team members?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Plus plan supports up to 4 team members and the Professional plan supports unlimited team members.

faqs.question: Does Scalenut offer a managed service option?
faqs.answer: Yes, its custom-priced VIP Service tier pairs dedicated GEO experts and AI agents to handle content strategy on the customer's behalf.

faqs.question: Which AI answer engines does Scalenut track?
faqs.answer: Scalenut tracks visibility across ChatGPT and Google AI Overviews on lower tiers, adding Perplexity tracking on the Professional plan.

use_cases.title: AI-driven content production at scale
use_cases.description: Content teams use Scalenut to research, draft, and optimize articles faster using AI assistance guided by competitor analysis.

use_cases.title: Agency content and SEO management
use_cases.description: Agencies use unlimited workspaces on the Professional plan to manage SEO and content for multiple client domains from one account.

use_cases.title: AI answer engine visibility tracking
use_cases.description: Brands monitor and optimize how often they are cited within ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity responses as AI search grows.
use_cases.audience: SEO teams and marketing leaders focused on AI search visibility

features: Content audit up to 400 pages per month
Track 100 AI prompts weekly (ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, Perplexity)
